nameMeanings.name: Núñez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Pedro, Jorge, Ramon.  Portuguese Guilherme, Joaquim, Vasco, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Núñez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Nuño (see Nuno ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Nuno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Salvador, Alfredo, Carlos, Francisco, JoseLuis, Miguel, Luis, Manuel, Alfonso.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo.
Spanish (Nuño), Portuguese, and Galician: from a medieval personal name, first attested in the Latin form Nunnus, of uncertain derivation.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guzmán
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Pedro, Rafael, Ramon, Raul.
Spanish (Guzmán): of uncertain and disputed etymology, probably from an ancient Germanic personal name. Compare De Guzman .
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Gusman  .

variants.name: De Guzman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ernesto, Pedro, Manuel, Reynaldo, Ricardo, Jaime, Alberto, Alfredo, Edgardo, Francisco, Mario.
Spanish (De Guzmán): patronymic from Guzmán (see Guzman ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gusman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Blanca, Jorge, Manuel, Salvador, Santiago, Adolfo, Alejandro, Alfonso, Alvino.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico; Gusmán): altered form of Spanish Guzmán (see Guzman ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name for a metalworker, from Yiddish gus ‘casting’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Leonor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Eléonore . See Leonora .

variants.name: Leonora
meanings: Shortened form of   Eleonora .

en.lifeSummary: señora de Medina Sidonia Leonor Núñez de Guzmán was born in 1310. She died in 1351, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: señora de Medina Sidonia Leonor Núñez de Guzmán est née en 1310. Elle est décédée en 1351, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: señora de Medina Sidonia Leonor Núñez de Guzmán wurde 1310 geboren. Sie starb 1351 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: señora de Medina Sidonia Leonor Núñez de Guzmán è nata il nel 1310. Morì nel 1351, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 41 anni.

es.lifeSummary: señora de Medina Sidonia Leonor Núñez de Guzmán nació en 1310. Murió en 1351, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 41 años.
